title: ‘The world of work is changing fast': Barely any workers think their job is safe as worries about AI refuse…
excerpt: Most workers fear AI, but it’s not actually replacing them
content_html:
• New data reveals that most workers fear AI’s potential impact on their jobs
  • Employees who feel secure in their roles are more productive and engaged
  • Employers must prioritize communication and invest in training and upskilling

New data from ADP’s People at Work 2026 report, which covers more than 39,000 working adults from 36 markets, confirms that most workers are still highly concerned about AI’s impact on their jobs, even though today’s tangible impacts are relatively minimal.

Only one in four UK workers strongly agree their job is safe from being replaced, and this drops to around one in five (21%) in Europe as a whole and 22% globally.

ADP’s report highlights a major disconnect between employment figures, which are generally pretty strong, and employees’ personal expectations about their roles’ futures.

Poor AI communication is creating a confidence gap

“The world of work is changing fast, and our findings reveal a gap between what the labour market is telling us and what employees are feeling,” ADP UK&NI SVP and GM Jeff Phipps said, noting that “employment is strong.”

The report, one of the most extensive of its kind, shows that confidence actually varies widely according to the nature of an employee’s role, with knowledge workers feeling more confident. Around one in three (34%) UK knowledge workers agree their jobs are safe, but only 19% of UK workers in repetitive roles feel secure.

But a sense of job security doesn’t just play into a worker’s satisfaction – it could also be impacting how they work, and the productivity that an employer sees. Workers who feel their jobs are secure are 6x more likely to be fully engaged at work and around 3x more likely to report high productivity.

Workers who feel secure are also around 2x as likely to say they have no intention of leaving their employer.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

More job security creates higher-performing workers

It seems that greater confidence about the future may enable employees to concentrate more on their work and raise their contributions, indicating a clear need for positive communication from management.

ADP advises employers to be transparent about any organizational and technological changes, and to explain how employees’ roles could be affected so that they don’t instantly assume their jobs could be at risk. The report also puts the responsibility of training and upskilling on employers.

“People want to know there's a place for them as their organisation evolves, and that they'll be supported to get there,” Phipps added.

The report also reveals that the bigger the company, the less confident a worker is likely to feel. Large corporations see a 22% confidence level, compared with 36% for mid-sized companies. In Europe, the inverse is true, proving that the world is still struggling to get to grips with AI communication and training regardless of geography or company size.

“The businesses that treat this seriously - investing in skills and being honest about change - are the ones seeing the payoff in how people perform and whether they stay,” Phipps said, urging companies to factor much more than mere tech implementation into their AI strategies as they risk losing workers.

Follow TechRadar on Google News and add us as a preferred source to get our expert news, reviews, and opinion in your feeds.

With several years’ experience freelancing in tech and automotive circles, Craig’s specific interests lie in technology that is designed to better our lives, including AI and ML, productivity aids, and smart fitness. He is also passionate about cars and the decarbonisation of personal transportation. As an avid bargain-hunter, you can be sure that any deal Craig finds is top value!

content_html_zh:
• 新数据显示，大多数工人担心人工智能对其工作的潜在影响
  • 对自己的角色感到安全的员工工作效率更高、参与度更高
  • 雇主必须优先考虑沟通并投资于培训和技能提升

ADP 的《2026 年工作人员报告》涵盖了来自 36 个市场的 39,000 多名在职成年人，该报告的新数据证实，大多数工人仍然高度关注人工智能对其工作的影响，尽管目前的实际影响相对较小。

只有四分之一的英国工人强烈认为他们的工作不会被取代，而整个欧洲这一比例下降到五分之一（21%）左右，全球这一比例下降到 22%。

ADP 的报告强调了就业数据（通常相当强劲）与员工对其职位未来的个人期望之间存在重大脱节。

人工智能沟通不畅正在造成信心差距

ADP UK&NI 高级副总裁兼总经理杰夫·菲普斯 (Jeff Phipps) 表示：“工作世界瞬息万变，我们的调查结果揭示了劳动力市场告诉我们的信息与员工的感受之间存在差距。”他指出，“就业形势强劲”。

该报告是同类报告中最广泛的报告之一，它表明，信心实际上根据员工角色的性质而存在很大差异，知识型员工感觉更自信。 大约三分之一 (34%) 的英国知识工作者认为他们的工作是安全的，但只有 19% 的英国重复性岗位员工感到安全。

但工作安全感不仅会影响员工的满意度，还可能影响他们的工作方式以及雇主眼中的生产力。 感觉工作有保障的员工全身心投入工作的可能性高出 6 倍，工作效率高的可能性高出约 3 倍。

感到安全的员工表示无意离开雇主的可能性也是普通员工的两倍左右。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

更多的工作保障创造更高绩效的员工

看来，对未来的信心增强可能会让员工更加专注于工作并提高贡献，这表明管理层显然需要积极的沟通。

ADP 建议雇主对任何组织和技术变革保持透明，并解释员工的角色可能受到的影响，以免他们立即认为自己的工作可能面临风险。 该报告还要求雇主承担培训和技能提升的责任。

“人们希望知道，随着组织的发展，他们能有一席之地，并且他们会得到支持以实现这一目标，”菲普斯补充道。

报告还显示，公司规模越大，员工的信心可能就越低。 大型企业的置信度为 22%，而中型企业的置信度为 36%。 在欧洲，情况恰恰相反，这证明无论地理位置或公司规模如何，世界仍然在努力掌握人工智能通信和培训。

菲普斯表示：“认真对待这一问题——投资于技能并诚实对待变革——的企业会从人们的表现以及他们是否留下来中看到回报。”他敦促企业在人工智能战略中考虑的不仅仅是技术实施，因为它们面临着失去员工的风险。

在 Google 新闻上关注 TechRadar，并将我们添加为首选来源，以便在您的 Feed 中获取我们的专家新闻、评论和意见。

克雷格在科技和汽车领域拥有多年的自由职业经验，他的具体兴趣在于旨在改善我们生活的技术，包括人工智能和机器学习、生产力辅助工具和智能健身。 他还热衷于汽车和个人交通的脱碳。 作为一名狂热的讨价还价者，您可以确信克雷格发现的任何交易都是超值的！
